operating_state: Total revenue increased 9.5% to $17.2 billion driven by organic growth across all segments, with commercial aerospace recovery continuing as aftermarket sales expanded reflecting increased flight hours and fleet utilization. Defense revenue grew modestly with higher F135 production and Advanced Technologies programs contributing to overall performance.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved to 20.7% from 20.1% as organic cost increases were offset by absence of prior-year Russia-related charges. Operating margin expanded to 9.6% from 6.9% driven by commercial aerospace recovery and elimination of prior-year impairments. Company-funded R&D decreased while customer-funded R&D increased.
material_events: Issued $3.0 billion in long-term debt to fund upcoming note maturities and general corporate purposes. Board elected Leanne G. Caret as independent director while reducing board size to thirteen directors. Announced organizational restructuring to consolidate four segments into three beginning July 2023. Repurchased $562 million of common stock and declared quarterly dividend of $0.59 per share.
macro_exposures: China formalized sanctions against RMD including fines and sanctioned CEO Gregory Hayes. Supply chain and labor constraints persist with materials shortages, delivery delays, and specialized personnel scarcity affecting production costs. Russia-Ukraine conflict maintains broad economic sanctions with Russian counter-sanctions affecting management team.
forward_outlook: Commercial aerospace recovery trajectory remains positive with long-term industry fundamentals intact despite uncertain timing for full capacity restoration. Defense bookings support backlog growth to $180 billion total. Management expects continued supply chain challenges with uncertain resolution timing and plans continued defense product sales to Taiwan despite potential future Chinese sanctions.
uncertainty_profile: EAC adjustments turned negative $124 million versus positive $36 million prior year across multiple programs. Impact of Chinese sanctions remains uncertain with potential for additional actions that could disrupt operations. Contract liabilities include advance payments from Middle East customer where regulatory approvals are no longer expected.
